title: hieracium murorum subsp. gaeopetum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. gaeopetum, also known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with medicinal uses including treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is rich in flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and immune-boosting effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Hieracium murorum subsp. gaeopetum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
